text: The surgeon switches to the right side of the patient, using ports in the right hypochondrium and iliac fossa to approach the splenic flexure. The left colon and rectum are mobilized with careful movements to avoid splenic injury. The inferior mesenteric artery is identified and high ligation is performed following a medial to lateral dissection. The rectum is mobilized, and the peritoneal reflection is divided, proceeding in the plane of total mesorectal excision to the middle to low rectal canal.

The surgeon switches to the right side of the patient, using ports in the right hypochondrium and iliac fossa to approach the splenic flexure. The left colon and rectum are mobilized with careful movements to avoid splenic injury. The inferior mesenteric artery is identified and high ligation is performed following a medial to lateral dissection. The laparoscopic procedure concludes with the mobilization of the rectum, dividing the peritoneal reflection and proceeding with total mesorectal excision to the middle to low rectal canal.

The laparoscopic view shows the perineal dissection being performed in a codocranial direction. The specimen is extracted through the perineal wound. The pelvic peritoneum is closed over a suction drain using an intracorporeal continuous suture.
